mechanism: Promotes apoptosis and differentiation of leukemic cells
additional_notes: Also known as ATO, commonly used in acute promyelocytic leukemia.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylase activity leading to cell cycle arrest and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA, targets dysregulated histones in cancer cells.

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase activity
additional_notes: Targets Philadelphia chromosome-positive leukemias.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple tyrosine kinases including BCR-ABL
additional_notes: Effective in leukemias resistant to Imatinib.

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 activity
additional_notes: Approved for myeloproliferative disorders.

mechanism: Targets VEGFR, PDGFR, and c-KIT
additional_notes: Used in the treatment of renal cell carcinoma and gastrointestinal stromal tumors.

mechanism: Disrupts proteasome activity leading to apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma.

mechanism: Targets and inhibits BCL-2 protein
additional_notes: Induces apoptosis in chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinases and other receptor tyrosine kinases
additional_notes: Used in the treatment of advanced renal cell carcinoma.

mechanism: Decreases hepatic glucose production and improves insulin sensitivity
additional_notes: Commonly used for Type 2 diabetes.

mechanism: Inhibits cholesterol synthesis pathway leading to cell cycle arrest
additional_notes: Includes simvastatin and lovastatin.

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal acidification impacting autophagic flux
additional_notes: Repurposed for several cancers due to its autophagy modulation.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase leading to oxidative stress
additional_notes: Originally used in rheumatoid arthritis.

mechanism: Inhibits ergosterol biosynthesis
additional_notes: Shows potential in inhibiting angiogenesis and cancer cell proliferation.

mechanism: Inhibits HIV protease and impacts various cellular pathways
additional_notes: Used in combination therapies for HIV.

mechanism: Interferes with DNA and RNA synthesis
additional_notes: Also being investigated for prion diseases.

mechanism: Disrupts cellular bioenergetics via oxidative phosphorylation uncoupling
additional_notes: Shows anticancer potential beyond its antiparasitic uses.

mechanism: Induces hypomethylation and reactivates silenced genes
additional_notes: Approved for certain myelodysplastic syndromes and AML.

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling pathway
additional_notes: Also known as Rapamycin, used in organ transplant rejection.

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal function and autophagosome-lysosome fusion
additional_notes: Similar mechanism to Chloroquine, also used in autoimmune diseases.
